title: Constructor Execution Order in Java
description: A flowchart illustrating the sequence and hierarchy of constructor calls when creating an object in an inheritance chain.
elements: Class is loaded (Static initializers run once)
relationships: The 'new' keyword in the main method triggers the child constructor
The first line of any constructor is an implicit call to super()
This creates a chain up the inheritance hierarchy to the Object constructor
After the super() call, instance initialization blocks run, followed by the rest of the constructor code
